rules.name: rules
rules.title: auditd (Linux Audit Daemon) rules aggregated on disk

control.name: control
control.title: auditd (Linux Audit Daemon) rule for a control
control.desc: We translate these into simple key-value pairs consisting of a flag and a value eg: --backlog_wait_time 60000  =>  {flag: "--backlog_wait_time", value: "60000"} eg: -b 8192                    =>  {flag: "-b", value: "8192"} eg: -D                         =>  {flag: "-D", value: nil}

file.name: file
file.title: auditd (Linux Audit Daemon) rule for a file

syscall.name: syscall
syscall.title: auditd (Linux Audit Daemon) rule for a syscall
syscall.desc: eg: -a always,exit -F arch=b32 -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset => { action: "always", list: "exit", syscalls: [], field_entries: [ key="arch" op="=" value="b32" key="auid" op=">=" value="1000" key="auid" op="!=" value="unset" ], keyname: nil, }

auditd.rule.control.name: auditd.rule.control

flag.name: flag
flag.title: The flag used for this control, i.e. the first part of the control including any leading `-`

value.name: value
value.title: The value of the control, which may be specified

auditd.rule.control.title: auditd (Linux Audit Daemon) rule for a control
auditd.rule.control.desc: We translate these into simple key-value pairs consisting of a flag and a value eg: --backlog_wait_time 60000  =>  {flag: "--backlog_wait_time", value: "60000"} eg: -b 8192                    =>  {flag: "-b", value: "8192"} eg: -D                         =>  {flag: "-D", value: nil}

keyname.title: The key name for related rules as specified by -k

permissions.name: permissions

auditd.rule.syscall.name: auditd.rule.syscall

comparisons.name: comparisons
comparisons.title: All inter-field comparisons as specified by -C

fields.name: fields

keyname.name: keyname
keyname.title: The key name for related rules as specified by -k

list.name: list
list.title: The list, the second value specified by -a

syscalls.name: syscalls

auditd.rule.syscall.title: auditd (Linux Audit Daemon) rule for a syscall
auditd.rule.syscall.desc: eg: -a always,exit -F arch=b32 -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset => { action: "always", list: "exit", syscalls: [], field_entries: [ key="arch" op="=" value="b32" key="auid" op=">=" value="1000" key="auid" op="!=" value="unset" ], keyname: nil, }
